pattern: Full rounds repeat with period equal to the total chalk per round, so reduce k modulo that sum and then walk the array once to find the first student whose chalk exceeds the remainder.
hints: The consumption pattern repeats every full pass, so only the leftover after complete passes matters.
Take k modulo the sum of all chalk values to skip the repeated passes in one step.
Scan from student 0, subtracting each chalk value, and return the first index where the remaining chalk is insufficient.

edge_inputs.description: k exactly equals one full round so student 0 replaces
k less than first student's chalk
k one less than full round, last student replaces

stress.description: random chalk arrays and large k forcing modular reduction
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 2000)
    chalk = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [chalk, k]

stress.description: small chalks so k spans many full rounds
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 500)
    chalk = [rng.randint(1, 5) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
    return [chalk, k]
